summary: Moved the expected MWCC compiler directory from the repository root into tools/mwcc_compiler and updated both Makefile tool paths and README build instructions accordingly. This was a small tooling/layout cleanup with no source decompilation changes.

key_files.reason: Updated CC and LD to invoke MWCC executables from tools/mwcc_compiler instead of mwcc_compiler at repository root.
Updated build instructions to tell contributors to place MWCC Build 158 PowerPC under tools/mwcc_compiler and added a note about CodeWarrior/GameCube compiler executables and licensing.

what_changed: Changed Makefile CC from $(WINE) mwcc_compiler/mwcceppc.exe to $(WINE) tools/mwcc_compiler/mwcceppc.exe.
Changed Makefile LD from $(WINE) mwcc_compiler/mwldeppc.exe to $(WINE) tools/mwcc_compiler/mwldeppc.exe.
Changed README build setup step so MWCC Build 158 PowerPC is placed in tools/mwcc_compiler rather than a root-level mwcc_compiler folder.
Expanded README wording to clarify that mwcceppc.exe, mwasmeppc.exe, and mwldeppc.exe can be installed with CodeWarrior 1.1 for GameCube, while the project does not provide a license or crack.
smart_moves: Kept third-party compiler artifacts under tools/, aligning the compiler location with other build tools such as tools/elf2dol.
Updated documentation in the same PR as the Makefile path change, reducing setup mismatch for contributors.
Explicitly documented the legal/access boundary for the proprietary CodeWarrior compiler instead of implying the project distributes it.
decomp_lessons: When relocating build dependencies, update both executable paths and contributor setup instructions in lockstep.
Proprietary compiler dependencies should be documented as user-provided artifacts, especially in decompilation projects where exact compiler behavior matters for matching.
Keeping external tools in a dedicated tools/ tree helps avoid cluttering the repository root and makes expected local setup easier to search.
naming_conventions: MWCC compiler directory is expected as tools/mwcc_compiler.
Compiler frontend executable path uses tools/mwcc_compiler/mwcceppc.exe.
README refers to the dependency as MWCC Build 158 PowerPC.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Check whether later PRs further normalize tool locations under tools/.
Search for any remaining root-level mwcc_compiler references after this PR.
Look for later README or setup script changes that clarify MWCC installation and licensing.
